what_it_does: Lists six file entries that assign claude-ds atomicity modules to wave-002 allocation batch 018 in the interpretive ledger.
observations: Each entry contains project, path, and root fields
All entries share the same project 'claude-ds' and root '/home/tim/company/design/claude-ds'
The paths are JavaScript files under the atomicity/ directory
No code symbols or file content are included
standouts: All six paths are in the atomicity subsystem: mode-engine, override, picker, scan-engine, shot, and vi-brain
It is a pure allocation manifest with no embedded code or symbols
conventions: Uses a JSON array of uniform objects with project, path, and root keys
Uses an absolute filesystem root paired with a relative path
concerns: No rationale or selection criteria for why these files are in batch-018
No timestamps, version, or content hashes
notes: This is a batch allocation manifest for six atomicity modules in the claude-ds project.
questions: What criteria place these specific files in batch-018?
What does wave-002 represent in the interpretive pipeline?
Will symbols or file content be added to this batch later?
purpose_vs_actual: The path marks it as an allocation batch, and the content is exactly a list of allocated files, so purpose and content align.
intention_signals: It is trying to stage a specific set of atomicity modules for an interpretive processing wave, creating a traceable assignment from files to a batch identifier.
contribution: It contributes a concrete, project-rooted file manifest that lets downstream processing know which atomicity modules are in scope for this batch without making cross-file claims.
summary_for_embedding: A JSON allocation batch listing six atomicity JavaScript files from the claude-ds project rooted at /home/tim/company/design/claude-ds.
